page_content:
MATH214 Elements of Probability and Statistics (3 Credits) Permutations and combinations; probability; collecting and representing data; using statistics to analyze and interpret data. Prerequisite: MATH212. Restriction: Must be in one of the following programs (Early Childhood Education; Special Education; Elementary Education; Middle School Education).

MATH240 Introduction to Linear Algebra (4 Credits) Basic concepts of linear algebra: vector spaces, applications to line and plane geometry, linear equations and matrices, similar matrices, linear transformations, eigenvalues, determinants and quadratic forms. Prerequisite: 1 course with a minimum grade of C- from (MATH131, MATH141). Credit Only Granted for: MATH240, MATH341, or MATH461.

MATH241 Calculus III (4 Credits) Introduction to multivariable calculus, including vectors and vector- valued functions, partial derivatives and applications of partial derivatives (such as tangent planes and Lagrange multipliers), multiple integrals, volume, surface area, and the classical theorems of Green, Stokes and Gauss. Prerequisite: Minimum grade of C- in MATH141. Credit Only Granted for: MATH241 or MATH340.
